title: Subitem default values
excerpt: Hi, item default values already available, please consider apply that default ability for subitems too.

posts.author: Kmk (kmk)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi, item default values already available, please consider apply that default ability for subitems too.

posts.author: benbloom (Benjamin Bloom)
posts.cookedHtml: I also need this… It would be great if you could reference the value of a parent item for default subitem. for example. when i have an item with the timeline column, having the date subitem default to the first date on the timeline would save a lot of clicks!

posts.author: Rooz (Rooz@Quad)
posts.cookedHtml:
I could use this also. we have a group called “Recurring meeting” where each person has one “meeting” tasks that occur each and every week with subtasks that break it down into all individual meetings. I use an automation task that duplicates this group each week. I was able to set the main tasks’ default date to “Current Date” so each week when the meeting tasks are duplicated, the date for the task is accurate for the week. There is no such feature for the subtasks so our Utilization Bubble report cannot use subtasks. It can only look at the main tasks.
Having default for subtasks solves this problem for me such that the Utilization report can show each person’s weekly bubble reflect subtasks as well based on their dates.

posts.author: Terry.Heard (Terry Heard)
posts.cookedHtml: Definitely Agree. Need to be able to rename the Subitem Column Label

posts.author: Fantasy-Media-Team (Fantasy Media Team)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey @egronberger, @benbloom, @Rooz, @Terry.Heard

We developed an app that solves this exact use case in 2 different ways.

First, setting default values based on the items name see below -

Screenshot 2024-01-10 at 10.51.082126×392 37.1 KB

Second, inheriting the date from the parent item -

Screenshot 2024-01-10 at 10.52.022026×412 40.7 KB

Third, just a default value based on creation date -

Screenshot 2024-01-10 at 10.51.472014×382 28.3 KB

We’re constantly adding new capabilities to it to make sure it’s easy and powerful to run automation on subitems just like it’s powerful on “regular” items.
You can find it in the monday.com marketplace in the platform or here - monday.com: Apps Marketplace

Hope that helps!

posts.author: Stephen.Ellis (Stephen)
posts.cookedHtml:
I would really like a way to reference a parent items value on the subitem.

For example, we have a requirements board where the parent item is all the information about a project (links to the requirements, estimates, which programme etc).
The subitem are then the tasks that are required to fulfil this project. These get assigned to teams and we build timeline views for the team based on these.
It would be great if when the user clicked the box on the timeline view, we could see the values from the parent item too - ie links to the requirements etc.

Additionally, when building a workload view, all the information about time and effort is on the subitem, but the programme is at the parent level so I cant build a report that shows effort per programme unless I manually copy the programme value to each subitem (which is a pain - even the “copy parent column” doesnt copy the actual value when creating a new subitem, it just copies the column type)
